revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23693191906|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== The {{tl|Whose}} template is a variant of the {{tl|Who}} template for statements with unclear referents, such as "Smith debated with Jones the validity of '''his''' theory of..." (Whose theory? Smith's, or Jones's?) Use good judgment when deciding whether greater specificity is actually in the best interests of the article. Words like ''some'' or ''most'' are not banned and can be useful and appropriate. If greater specificity would result in a tedious [[wikt:laundry list|laundry list]] of items with no real importance, then Wikipedia should remain concise, even if it means being vague. If the reliable sources are not specific—if the reliable sources say only that "In some countries..."—then Wikipedia must remain vague. You may use either one of the following formats: : {{whose|{{subst:DATE}}}} or : {{whose|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} This tag will categorize tagged articles into [[:Category:Articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases]]. This [[Wikipedia:Template messages|template]] is a [[Wikipedia:Self-references to avoid|self-reference]] and is part of the [[Wikipedia:WikiProject|Wikipedia project]] rather than the [[:en:encyclopedia|encyclopedic]] content. ==See also== * {{tl|Peacock}} (message box) * {{tl|Weasel}} (message box) * [[Wikipedia:Avoid weasel words]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup#Neutrality and factual accuracy]], including boxes flagging weasel words, as well as inline templates such as {{tl|Who}} and {{tl|When}} {{Inline tags}} ==TemplateData== { "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The current month and year; for instance, \"June 2014\" (without the quotes)", "type": "string", "autovalue": "May 2025", "suggested": true } }, "description": "A template to tag attributions or pronouns with unclear referents.", "format": "inline" } {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Inline cleanup templates]] [[Category:Neutrality templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
